case.title: FA875026S7002 CORTEX Presolicitation Review
case.question: A public SAM.gov presolicitation arrived. Should we pursue it?
case.subtitle: AI FlowOps processed the public package as a pre-bid capture decision, routed specialist reviews, synthesized the conditions, and produced a BD/Ops pursue-with-conditions decision.

intake_automation.headline: Overnight AI screening promoted this presolicitation before the case opened.
intake_automation.summary: The system monitored public opportunity records, downloaded the available SAM.gov package, normalized the PDFs, enriched the packet with capture context, and used AI to decide that this opportunity deserved specialist pursuit review.
intake_automation.enrichment_context_added: Opportunity stage, document roles, capture-readiness context, department routing, and grounded source evidence.
screening_reasons: The package describes a plausible technical opportunity requiring capture analysis.
The presolicitation contains enough evidence to route Legal, Security, Finance, and Implementation review.
The correct near-term decision is whether to pursue and prepare, not whether to submit a final bid.

stages.label: Document received
stages.title: What came in?
stages.body: A public SAM.gov presolicitation package was downloaded, normalized, and promoted into a capture-readiness review.

stages.label: AI extraction
stages.title: What did AI read from the presolicitation?
stages.body: The AI extracted grounded facts and 15 evidence phrases from the public package.

stages.label: AI recommendation
stages.title: What did AI recommend - and who did it call?
stages.body: The AI recommended pursuit with conditions and routed specialist packets.

stages.label: Department routing
stages.title: What did AI recommend - and who did it call?
stages.body: Four department packets were created for pursuit-readiness review.

stages.label: Specialist reviews
stages.title: What did the specialists decide?
stages.body: Human reviewers evaluated the AI packets and returned pursuit conditions.

stages.label: AI synthesis
stages.title: What did AI synthesize for BD/Ops?
stages.body: The AI combined specialist reviews into a capture decision packet.

stages.label: BD/Ops decision
stages.title: What did BD/Ops decide?
stages.body: BD/Ops logged the final pursue-with-conditions decision.

received_summary.label: Stage
received_summary.detail: Pursuit decision, not final bid submission

extraction_time_saved.headline: AI converted the public package into a routed capture-review packet before humans started reading.

extraction_cards.label: Primary route
extraction_cards.detail: Eligibility, data rights, OCI, export-control gates

extracted_json.decision_question: Should BD/Ops pursue and prepare for the next solicitation step?

routing_logic_intro: The AI treated this as a presolicitation capture decision. It did not ask whether to submit a final bid today; it asked which departments need to qualify the pursuit before BD/Ops invests more capture effort.

routing_logic.reason: Legal should qualify both FAR and OT paths before deeper capture investment because the government can choose the instrument later and negotiate terms and conditions with selectees.; This is a conditional eligibility blocker. If the planned prime, subcontractor, or staffing model relies on foreign participation, the opportunity is not pursuit-ready.

If the proposed solution stores government or government-related data in the cloud, pursuit should continue only with a DISA-authorized hosting path at the required level.; Security readiness is a near-term gate because the solicitation expects a BASIC NIST SP 800-171 SPRS assessment before deeper pursuit investment is justified.

Finance should stage-gate pursuit spend because the agency expressly preserves a zero-award outcome and warns that awards depend on funding fluctuations.; If the team lacks significant nontraditional or nonprofit participation, Finance may need to support at least one-third non-federal cost share before this is capture-ready.

This is a gated presolicitation pursuit, not an open full-proposal bid. The immediate capture question is whether the team can produce a competitive white paper first and earn invitation into the next step.; The FY26 suggested white-paper window has already passed. Pursuit is still possible, but capture should treat this as a later-cycle effort and align to the FY27 timing unless the customer indicates otherwise.

departments.name: Legal
facts: Legal should qualify both FAR and OT paths before deeper capture investment because the government can choose the instrument later and negotiate terms and conditions with selectees.
This is a conditional eligibility blocker. If the planned prime, subcontractor, or staffing model relies on foreign participation, the opportunity is not pursuit-ready.
This is a presubmission gate that can become a hard stop. Any FOCI-affected entity needs approved mitigation paperwork before a white paper or proposal can be submitted.
If the pursuit depends on non-U.S.-citizen or non-permanent-resident personnel, export-control approval becomes a pre-bid gate with explicit civil and criminal penalty exposure.
departments.packet_precis: Legal sees this as worth pursuing for capture review if the listed legal gates are resolved before any final bid commitment.
packet_questions: Are any eligibility, SETA/A&AS, OCI, or foreign-participation limits disqualifying?
Can the company accept the expected SBIR/ARA data-rights and export-control terms?
departments.summary: Legal sees this as worth pursuing for capture review if the listed legal gates are resolved before any final bid commitment.
conditions: Confirm eligibility, award vehicle fit, data-rights terms, OCI exposure, foreign participation limits, and export-control registration before final bid commitment.

departments.name: Security
facts: If the proposed solution stores government or government-related data in the cloud, pursuit should continue only with a DISA-authorized hosting path at the required level.
Security readiness is a near-term gate because the solicitation expects a BASIC NIST SP 800-171 SPRS assessment before deeper pursuit investment is justified.
Some work may require cleared facilities and already-cleared personnel at award, so the opportunity is risky if the company lacks a credible classified-delivery path.
departments.packet_precis: Security sees this as worth pursuing if clearance, cloud, and data-handling requirements are feasible.
packet_questions: Does the delivery team already have the facility/personnel clearance posture required?
Will any cloud service store or process government or government-related data?
departments.summary: Security sees this as worth pursuing if clearance, cloud, and data-handling requirements are feasible.
conditions: Confirm clearance posture, safeguarding obligations, DISA/cloud authorization needs, and export-controlled data handling before proposal resources scale.

departments.name: Finance
facts: Finance should stage-gate pursuit spend because the agency expressly preserves a zero-award outcome and warns that awards depend on funding fluctuations.
If the team lacks significant nontraditional or nonprofit participation, Finance may need to support at least one-third non-federal cost share before this is capture-ready.
A cost-reimbursement path cannot be awarded without a government-approved accounting system, making finance-readiness a presolicitation gate rather than a post-selection task.
Playbook rule 'unusual_penalty_terms' matched: Non-standard penalty terms need finance review.
departments.packet_precis: Finance sees this as worth pursuing if cost-share and accounting requirements fit the expected opportunity value.
packet_questions: Does the company qualify for reduced cost-share treatment?
Is a government-approved accounting system already in place or obtainable in time?
departments.summary: Finance sees this as worth pursuing if cost-share and accounting requirements fit the expected opportunity value.
conditions: Model cost-share exposure, funding uncertainty, and accounting-system readiness before authorizing full proposal spend.

departments.name: Implementation
facts: This is a gated presolicitation pursuit, not an open full-proposal bid. The immediate capture question is whether the team can produce a competitive white paper first and earn invitation into the next step.

The FY26 suggested white-paper window has already passed. Pursuit is still possible, but capture should treat this as a later-cycle effort and align to the FY27 timing unless the customer indicates otherwise.

Any deliverable that relies on third-party software will need disclosure, licensing, and contracting-officer approval, so implementation should validate dependency and licensing strategy before the next solicitation step.
departments.packet_precis: Implementation sees this as worth pursuing if technical scope and staffing assumptions remain plausible during capture.
packet_questions: Can the team support the likely SDR gateware, firmware, and cyber-control work?
Does the presolicitation provide enough scope to estimate capture and delivery risk?
departments.summary: Implementation sees this as worth pursuing if technical scope and staffing assumptions remain plausible during capture.
conditions: Confirm technical scope fit, staffing assumptions, and feasibility of SDR, firmware, cyber-control, or integration work before bid commitment.

ai_synthesis.headline: Recommend pursue with conditions - prepare for the next solicitation step.
ai_synthesis.summary: Pursue this presolicitation as a capture effort, not a near-term full-bid commitment. The immediate objective is a competitive white paper for a later cycle, but BD/Ops should only scale pursuit after resolving eligibility, security, and finance gates that could become hard blockers.

document_sections.heading: Opportunity summary
document_sections.body: FA875026S7002 is a gated AFRL/ARA-style presolicitation where white papers are the current action and formal proposals are by invitation only. The FY26 suggested white-paper date has passed, but submissions remain possible through December 31, 2030, with later-year funding uncertainty. The opportunity appears technically plausible, but capture readiness depends on foreign participation limits, FOCI/OCI posture, export-control constraints, clearance/cloud compliance, and cost-share/accounting fit.

document_sections.heading: Specialist summary
document_sections.body: Legal supports pursuit with conditions because award instrument, eligibility, OCI, foreign participation, export control, and data-rights posture must be qualified before any bid commitment. Security supports pursuit with conditions because classified-clearance, SPRS/NIST readiness, and any DISA-authorized cloud path must be confirmed before proposal resources scale. Finance supports pursuit with conditions because zero-award risk, possible one-third cost share, and approved-accounting-system requirements could materially affect ROI and readiness. Implementation supports pursuit because the current step is a white paper, but technical scope, staffing, schedule realism, and third-party software/licensing dependencies still need capture validation.

document_sections.heading: Rationale
document_sections.body: The evidence supports continued pursuit, but not an unconditional green light. This is a presolicitation with an invitation-only second step, so the correct near-term decision is whether to invest in capture and prepare a white paper. That favors pursuit because the opportunity is still open for later cycles and implementation believes the scope is plausibly supportable. However, several issues could become hard blockers if left unresolved: foreign participation is prohibited, FOCI mitigation may be required before submission, OCI failures can eliminate the team, export-control approvals may be needed for certain personnel, some work may require cleared facilities and personnel at award, cloud use may require DISA authorization, cost share may be mandatory absent significant nontraditional/nonprofit participation, and a government-approved accounting system may be required for some award paths. Those are serious but currently conditional rather than proven disqualifiers, so the best synthesis is pursue_with_conditions rather than hold_pending_information or do_not_pursue.

document_sections.heading: Open questions
document_sections.body: Is the intended prime/sub team fully eligible under the foreign participation restriction, and are any FOCI issues present? Are any OCI, SETA/A&AS, or related affiliation issues disqualifying for this team? Can the company accept the likely openness and government-advantageous data-rights posture without undermining core IP strategy? Will the planned staffing model require any non-U.S.-citizen or non-permanent-resident personnel? Does the delivery team already have the facility and personnel clearances required for the likely scope? Will any proposed architecture store or process government or government-related data in the cloud? Does the company already have a BASIC NIST SP 800-171 SPRS assessment and the needed safeguarding controls? Does the team qualify for reduced cost-share treatment through significant nontraditional or nonprofit participation? Is a government-approved accounting system already available for any cost-reimbursement award path? Is there enough technical scope clarity to estimate capture cost and delivery risk credibly for the next cycle? Can the team support the likely SDR, firmware, cyber-control, and integration workload with realistic staffing? Will any critical deliverable depend on third-party software that could complicate licensing or contracting-officer approval?

ai_synthesis.note: The AI synthesis recommends; BD/Ops owns the final capture decision.

conditions.text: Treat this as a capture/white-paper pursuit only until invited forward; do not authorize full proposal spend yet.
Confirm the company and team structure comply with the foreign participation prohibition.
If any entity has FOCI exposure, obtain approved mitigation documentation before submitting a white paper.
Complete prime/subcontractor OCI screening and confirm mitigation support is credible.
Confirm whether any non-U.S.-citizen or non-permanent-resident personnel are needed; if so, determine whether export-control approvals are realistically obtainable.
Qualify acceptable award-vehicle and data-rights posture, including whether government-favorable openness/data-rights terms are commercially acceptable.
Confirm whether the target solution would require cloud storage or processing of government or government-related data; if yes, map to a DISA-authorized hosting path at the required level.
Verify BASIC NIST SP 800-171 assessment posture in SPRS and overall safeguarding readiness.

audit_events.event: AI intake - Government PDFs extracted and normalized into a real-case packet.
BD/Ops Lead - BD/Ops logged final decision: pursue_with_conditions.

evidence_items.source_phrase: Only white papers will be accepted as initial submissions; formal proposals will be accepted by invitation only.
evidence_items.full_source: Only white papers will be accepted as initial submissions; formal proposals will be accepted by invitation only.
evidence_items.extracted_fact: The immediate pursuit step is a white paper, and full proposal access depends on government invitation.
evidence_items.ai_reasoning: This is a gated presolicitation pursuit, not an open full-proposal bid. The immediate capture question is whether the team can produce a competitive white paper first and earn invitation into the next step.
evidence_items.specialist_conclusion: Specialist review accepted pursuit with conditions.
